summary: Crystal‑clear audience recording captures a blistering Chicago ’83 show with a scorching Bertha opener, rippin’ Sugaree and a legendary Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence.
review: The tape is praised as one of the cleanest audience (AUD) recordings ever posted – crisp, well‑balanced and expertly transferred by Charlie Miller. Musically the concert is described as “smoking” from start to finish, with a tight first set highlighted by Bertha, Cold Rain & Snow, Sugaree and Scarlet > Fire, and an explosive second set featuring a standout Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s and extended Bird Song jams. Overall the performance is rated as exceptional despite one outlier note about occasional lyrical flubs.

ai_rating.rationale: Eight of nine detailed reviews give 5‑star praise for both the show and recording; only one low rating is an outlier, so the weighted average yields a near‑perfect score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean sound with a high‑quality Charlie Miller transfer; occasional minor lyrical flubs on Dupree's but overall clear mix and good balance between instruments and vocals.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as refined and tight, second set rock‑filled and high‑energy; transitions (e.g., Scarlet > Fire) are praised and the Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence caps a superb second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note his blazing solos, ability to cover lyrical slips (Sugaree, Fire), and an overall stellar vocal/guitar performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is highlighted as loud and powerful (‘Phil Bombs’, ‘louder than loud’), driving the groove throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for energetic vocals on Estimated and a strong presence after a long break; no major criticisms.
band_member_comments.Keys: Unique keyboard textures noted (Mutron in Duprees) and overall solid support, though no specific complaints.
band_member_comments.Drums: Percussion described as nice and propulsive; Bird Song’s extended jam showcases the drums well.
